F3 Rule 22.12(2) omitted (22.4.2014) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No.3) Rules 2013 (S.I. 2013/3204) , rules 1 , 68 (with rule 137 ); S.I. 2014/954 , art. 2

### Form of affidavit E+W


22.13. An affidavit (GL) must comply with the requirements set out in the Practice Direction 22A.


### Affidavit made outside the jurisdiction E+W


22.14. A person may make an affidavit (GL) outside the jurisdiction in accordance with—


### Notice to admit facts E+W


22.15. —(1) A party may serve notice on another party requiring the other party to admit the facts, or the part of the case of the serving party, specified in the notice.


(2) A notice to admit facts must be served no later than 21 days before the final hearing.


(3) Where the other party makes any admission in answer to the notice, the admission may be used against that party only—


(4) The court may allow a party to amend or withdraw any admission made by that party on such terms as it thinks just.


### Notice to admit or produce documents E+W


22.16. —(1) A party to whom a document is disclosed is deemed to admit the authenticity of that document unless notice is served by that party that the party wishes the document to be proved at the final hearing.


(2) A notice to prove a document must be served—


### Notarial acts and instruments E+W


22.17. A notarial act or instrument may be received in evidence without further proof as duly authenticated in accordance with the requirements of law unless the contrary is proved.
